growth_records.medium: Modified MR medium (phosphate-enhanced, TMS removed, N source removed) used as catholyte; formate (from CO2 electrolysis) as sole carbon source

up.units: mg/h (volumetric supply=consumption at controlled concentration; ~0.131 g/L/h in 2 L)
up.method: calculated

up.units: mmol/gDCW/h (approx specific, on 120 h total DCW)
up.method: calculated

sec.units: mg/h (volumetric, avg over 120 h in 2 L)
mg/gDCW/h (~0.080 mmol C4H6O2/gDCW/h, approx specific on 120 h total DCW)

growth_records.cite: Lim et al. Biohybrid CO2 electrolysis for the direct synthesis of polyesters from CO2. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2221438120. [PMC10083616]
growth_records.snippet: This system produced 1.38 g PHB with a PHB content of 83.0% of DCW at 120 h. The average PHB productivity during 120 h of operation was 11.5 mg h-1... The initial OD600 was 0.9, which corresponded to an initial DCW of 0.2 g DCW L-1... forma
growth_records.notes: C. necator H16, formate as sole carbon source (from CO2 electrolysis), fed-batch 120 h, 30°C, air-purged (aerobic). GROWTH RATE: non-PHB biomass increased 0.40 g -> 0.56 g over first 24 h; mu = ln(0.56/0.40)/24 = ln(1.4)/24 = 0.3365/24 = 0.0140/h; td = ln2/0.0140 = 49.4 h (growth is formate-limited/near-stationary, PHB-accumulating). (OD-based apparent mu incl. PHB = ln(2.7/0.9)/48 = 0.023/h.) FORMATE UPTAKE: electrolyzer produces formate at 262 mg/h into 2 L; concentration held 0.26-1.0 g/L so consumption ~= supply = 262 mg/h volumetric. Total DCW at 120 h = 1.38 g PHB / 0.83 = 1.66 g in 2 L (0.83 g/L). Specific qFormate = 262 mg/h / 1.66 gDCW = 158 mg/gDCW/h; /46.03 g/mol = 3.42 mmol/gDCW/h. PHB: reported volumetric productivity 11.5 mg/h; specific = 11.5/1.66 = 6.9 mg/gDCW/h; /86.09 g/mol (C4H6O2 monomer) = 0.080 mmol/gDCW/h. YIELD: total formate supplied = 262 mg/h x 120 h = 31.44 g; Y(PHB/formate) = 1.38/31.44 = 0.044 g/g (approx; assumes near-complete formate consumption). Specific rates are approximate (biomass changes over run; used 120 h total DCW) -> confidence medium. pH not stated (catholyte 30 g/L KH2PO4 + 4 g/L K2HPO4 buffered). Seed culture (LB, 30°C, 250 rpm) reported without a growth rate, not tabulated.

growth_records.medium: Modified MR medium (phosphate-enhanced, TMS removed, N source removed) catholyte; formate sole carbon source (steady-state/semi-continuous operation)

up.method: reported

growth_records.cite: Lim et al. Biohybrid CO2 electrolysis for the direct synthesis of polyesters from CO2. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2023). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2221438120. [PMC10083616]
growth_records.snippet: During the 18 d of operation, the electrochemical production of formate was stably conducted... and PHB was continually produced to a total of 1.45 g... during the continuous mode, the PHB contents were maintained within a range of 30~40 wt
growth_records.notes: Distinct condition: steady-state/semi-continuous biohybrid run (Fig. 4), 18 days, 30°C, aerobic. Cells and medium exchanged manually every 24 h to maintain constant cell density and constant formate/PHB concentrations ('similar to a chemostat'); classified culture_mode=chemostat but NO dilution rate reported and exchange is manual/semi-continuous, so growth_rate not derivable (left null). Formate supply 262 mg/h (reported). PHB total 1.45 g cumulative; steady-state PHB content 30-40 wt% (up to 53.7 wt%). No specific mu/qS/qP computable from reported data at constant cell density -> confidence low. Second record for same paper (distinct operating mode from the 120 h fed-batch record).

growth_records.cite: Dronsella et al. One-carbon fixation via the synthetic reductive glycine pathway exceeds yield of the Calvin cycle. Nature Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41564-025-01941-9. [PMC11879842]
growth_records.snippet: Strain CRG6 clearly grew faster than CRG4 at a doubling time in batch cultures of ~11 h, which was (still) slower than that of the wild-type CBB cycle strain (~6 h doubling time; Fig. 2a).
growth_records.notes: Wild-type CBB cycle strain (H16 delta-phaC1) formatotrophic batch growth, doubling time ~6 h reported in text (Fig 2a). mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/6 = 0.1155 h^-1 (rounded 0.116). Sole substrate = 80 mM sodium formate; bicarbonate supplied only in batch to ensure sufficient CO2. No qS or product secretion quantified in batch.

growth_records.cite: Dronsella et al. One-carbon fixation via the synthetic reductive glycine pathway exceeds yield of the Calvin cycle. Nature Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41564-025-01941-9. [PMC11879842]
growth_records.snippet: Strain CRG6 clearly grew faster than CRG4 at a doubling time in batch cultures of ~11 h, which was (still) slower than that of the wild-type CBB cycle strain (~6 h doubling time; Fig. 2a).
growth_records.notes: Engineered rGlyP strain CRG6 formatotrophic batch growth, doubling time ~11 h reported in text. mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/11 = 0.0630 h^-1. Sole substrate = 80 mM sodium formate; CRG strains additionally deleted for RuBisCO (delta-cbbSL2, delta-cbbSLp). No qS or secretion quantified in batch.

growth_records.medium: M9 minimal

up.method: calculated qS = mu/Yxs = 0.05 h^-1 / 3.88 gCDW mol^-1 = 0.01289 mol/gCDW/h = 12.89 mmol/gCDW/h

yields.Yxs: 3.88 +/- 0.19 g CDW / mol formate (measured, chemostat steady state); FBA-predicted 3.92 g CDW / mol formate

growth_records.cite: Dronsella et al. One-carbon fixation via the synthetic reductive glycine pathway exceeds yield of the Calvin cycle. Nature Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41564-025-01941-9. [PMC11879842]
growth_records.snippet: we obtained biomass yields of 3.88 +/- 0.19 g CDW mol-1 for the CBB cycle strain and 4.52 +/- 0.17 g CDW mol-1 for the rGlyP strain ... in bioreactors in chemostat mode at a dilution rate of 0.05 h-1 (14 h doubling time).
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state: at steady state mu = D = 0.05 h^-1 (td = ln2/0.05 = 13.86 h, reported as ~14 h). Measured biomass yield on formate Yxs = 3.88 g CDW/mol formate (n=9 steady-state samples). Formate is sole carbon+energy source. Formate uptake qS derived: qS = mu/Yxs; convert Yxs to gCDW per mmol = 3.88/1000 = 0.00388 gCDW/mmol; qS = 0.05/0.00388 = 12.89 mmol formate/gCDW/h. No residual formate detected by HPLC (all consumed). No organic-acid secretion detected (HPLC screened lactate/acetate/propionate/isobutyrate/butyrate).

growth_records.strain: CRG6 delta-dadA6 (genome-integrated reductive glycine pathway, glycine oxidase deleted)
growth_records.medium: M9 minimal

up.method: calculated qS = mu/Yxs = 0.05 h^-1 / 4.52 gCDW mol^-1 = 0.01106 mol/gCDW/h = 11.06 mmol/gCDW/h

yields.Yxs: 4.52 +/- 0.17 g CDW / mol formate (measured, chemostat steady state; 16.6% higher than CBB wild type); FBA-predicted 4.54 g CDW / mol formate

growth_records.cite: Dronsella et al. One-carbon fixation via the synthetic reductive glycine pathway exceeds yield of the Calvin cycle. Nature Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41564-025-01941-9. [PMC11879842]
growth_records.snippet: we obtained biomass yields of 3.88 +/- 0.19 g CDW mol-1 for the CBB cycle strain and 4.52 +/- 0.17 g CDW mol-1 for the rGlyP strain, showing a 16.6% higher yield compared with the wild type in the same condition.
growth_records.notes: Engineered rGlyP strain (CRG6 delta-dadA6) chemostat steady state: mu = D = 0.05 h^-1 (td ~14 h). Measured Yxs = 4.52 g CDW/mol formate (n=9). Formate sole substrate. qS derived: qS = mu/Yxs = 0.05 / (4.52/1000) = 0.05/0.00452 = 11.06 mmol formate/gCDW/h. rGlyP uses less formate per biomass than CBB (higher yield => lower qS). No residual formate (HPLC below detection); no organic-acid secretion detected.

growth_records.cite: Dronsella et al. One-carbon fixation via the synthetic reductive glycine pathway exceeds yield of the Calvin cycle. Nature Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1038/s41564-025-01941-9. [PMC11879842]
growth_records.snippet: At the end of the chemostat experiment, the dilution rate was increased to 0.087 h-1, which corresponds to 8 h of doubling time. Both strains were maintained at this dilution rate.

growth_records.medium: not stated

up.method: growth on formate as sole C/energy source (rate not quantified here)

growth_records.cite: Orsi, Enrico et al. Synergistic investigation of natural and synthetic C1-trophic microorganisms to foster a circular carbon economy. Nature Communications (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41467-023-42166-w. [PMC10590403]
growth_records.snippet: While C. necator can naturally grow on formate (through formate oxidation and the CBB cycle) with a doubling time of 3 h, the fastest synthetic formatotrophic strain to date has a doubling time of 6 h.
growth_records.notes: Doubling time td = 3 h for wild-type C. necator growing on formate is CITED from ref 88 within this Perspective; not measured here. Derived mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/3 = 0.231 h^-1. The fastest SYNTHETIC formatotrophic strain (td = 6 h, mu = ln2/6 = 0.116 h^-1, ref 89) is an engineered strain and is not recorded as a separate natural-prokaryote record. No medium/uptake/secretion quantification given.

growth_records.medium: JMM minimal medium + 40 mM sodium glycolate

up.method: not quantified as a specific rate; 40 mM sodium glycolate sole carbon source

growth_records.cite: Claassens, Nico J. et al. Phosphoglycolate salvage in a chemolithoautotroph using the Calvin cycle. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2012288117. [PMC7486775]
growth_records.snippet: a wild-type C. necator could efficiently grow on glycolate (doubling time of 3.2 ± 0.1 h) (WT in Fig. 2A). Growth experiments were conducted in 96-well plate readers in minimal medium (JMM) supplemented with 40 mM sodium glycolate.
growth_records.notes: Reported doubling time td=3.2 +/- 0.1 h (biological triplicates). mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/3.2 = 0.2166 /h. Temperature 30 C (Materials and Methods). pH of JMM not stated in text. Medium named JMM (a defined minimal medium, composition given in cited ref 49, not in this article) so medium_composition_stated=false; only carbon source (40 mM sodium glycolate, Sigma-Aldrich) is explicit. No quantitative uptake/secretion rates reported for this condition. Deletion-strain doubling times on the same 40 mM glycolate medium (same condition, engineered strains, not emitted as separate WT records): DeltaGP 7.9 h, DeltaGP DeltaC2 9.6 h, DeltaGP DeltaOX 22 h, DeltaGP DeltaC2 DeltaOX 27 h, DeltaC2 DeltaOX DeltaMC 3.4 h, DeltaRub 3.3 h; DeltaGDH and DeltaGP DeltaMC = no growth.

up.method: not quantified; 40 mM sodium oxalate sole carbon source

growth_records.cite: Claassens, Nico J. et al. Phosphoglycolate salvage in a chemolithoautotroph using the Calvin cycle. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2012288117. [PMC7486775]
growth_records.snippet: while a wild-type C. necator grew efficiently on oxalate (doubling time of 6.5 ± 0.1 h) (SI Appendix, Fig. S1), growth of the ApbA2 strain on this carbon source was impaired (doubling time of 10 ± 1.1 h)
growth_records.notes: Reported doubling time td=6.5 +/- 0.1 h (SI Appendix Fig. S1). mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/6.5 = 0.1066 /h. 30 C. Medium JMM (composition in cited ref, not in article) + 40 mM sodium oxalate (Sigma-Aldrich). No quantitative uptake/secretion rates. Engineered-strain doubling times on same oxalate medium (not emitted separately): DeltaapbA2 10 +/- 1.1 h, glycerate-pathway KO 13 +/- 0.7 h, Deltafrc-oxc = no growth.

sec.method: WT does not accumulate glycolate; a DeltaGDH (glycolate dehydrogenase) strain excretes glycolate into medium (measured by ion chromatography, Fig. 5); no numeric rate for WT

growth_records.cite: Claassens, Nico J. et al. Phosphoglycolate salvage in a chemolithoautotroph using the Calvin cycle. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2012288117. [PMC7486775]
growth_records.snippet: Autotrophic growth of wild-type C. necator under these conditions resulted in a much lower growth rate than observed at 10% CO2 (doubling time of 21 ± 0.7 vs. ∼3 h). Growth experiments were conducted in 700-mL bioreactor cultures on minimal
growth_records.notes: Autotrophic H2-oxidizing growth at ambient CO2 (~0.04%). Reported td=21 +/- 0.7 h (biological duplicates). mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/21 = 0.0330 /h. 30 C, 700-mL bioreactor, 6.25 L/min sparge of ambient air + 4% H2. No quantitative gas uptake or product secretion rates measured. Glycolate secretion demonstrated only for the DeltaGDH deletion strain (Fig. 5, ion chromatography), following prior reports (refs 24,25); WT and other strains showed no detectable glyoxylate in medium. Engineered-strain autotrophic ambient-CO2 doubling times (same condition, not emitted separately): DeltaC2 DeltaOX DeltaMC 20 +/- 2 h, DeltaGP 35 +/- 1 h, DeltaGP DeltaC2 DeltaOX 31 +/- 1 h; DeltaGP DeltaMC = no growth.

growth_records.cite: Claassens, Nico J. et al. Phosphoglycolate salvage in a chemolithoautotroph using the Calvin cycle. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A (2020). DOI:10.1073/pnas.2012288117. [PMC7486775]
growth_records.snippet: Autotrophic growth of wild-type C. necator ... a much lower growth rate than observed at 10% CO2 (doubling time of 21 ± 0.7 vs. ∼3 h) ... control experiments were conducted at 10% CO2 + 4% hydrogen (supplemented with air).
growth_records.notes: Autotrophic H2-oxidizing growth at elevated 10% CO2 (control condition). Reported doubling time approximate (~3 h). mu = ln2/td = 0.6931/3 = 0.231 /h (approximate, since td reported as '~3 h'). 30 C, 700-mL bioreactor, 6.25 L/min sparge of 10% CO2 + 4% H2 + air. No quantitative gas uptake or secretion rates. At 10% CO2, DeltaGP DeltaMC grew like WT (phosphoglycolate salvage negligible), confirming the ~3 h WT doubling time is not salvage-limited.

growth_records.cite: Gascoyne, Joshua Luke et al. Engineering Cupriavidus necator H16 for the autotrophic production of (R)-1,3-butanediol. Metabolic Engineering (2021). DOI:10.1016/j.ymben.2021.06.010. [PMC8449065]
growth_records.method: not reported numerically (paper notes only a qualitative decrease in growth rate under C:N=50 vs C:N=6; specific growth rate mu computed via Widdel 2007 eq but no value stated in text)
growth_records.snippet: Cumulative yields of (R)-1,3-BDO were steady for the duration of 96-h cell growth period ranging from 0.035 to 0.055 Cmol Cmol-1 ... highest yield of 0.055 +/- 0.003 Cmol Cmol-1 obtained using strain H16DeltaC-p2 ... large amounts of pyruva
growth_records.notes: Engineered strain, heterotrophic nitrogen-limited batch shake-flask. Values are carbon YIELDS (Cmol/Cmol) measured by HPLC, NOT rates in mmol/gDW/h; secretion is experimental (is_experimental:true). Growth rate and substrate uptake rate not reported numerically, so mu/qS cannot be computed (no biomass-vs-time or Yxs given in text; only DCW time-course in Fig 2 which is a figure). Pyruvate 0.419+/-0.003 Cmol/Cmol is verbatim for H16DeltaC-p2. 1,3-BDO 0.055+/-0.003 is the max single 24-h period yield. Base MM per Methods (1 g/L NH4Cl for C:N=6; reduced to 0.6 g/L for NLMM C:N=50) plus 9 g/L Na2HPO4.12H2O, 1.5 g/L KH2PO4, 0.2 g/L MgSO4.7H2O, ferric ammonium citrate, SL7 trace.

yields.carbon_to_1,3BDO: up to 40% Cmol/Cmol of carbon directed to (R)-1,3-BDO

growth_records.cite: Gascoyne, Joshua Luke et al. Engineering Cupriavidus necator H16 for the autotrophic production of (R)-1,3-butanediol. Metabolic Engineering (2021). DOI:10.1016/j.ymben.2021.06.010. [PMC8449065]
growth_records.snippet: produced 2.97 g/L of (R)-1,3-BDO and achieved production rate of nearly 0.4 Cmol Cmol-1 h-1 autotrophically. This is first report of (R)-1,3-BDO production from CO2 ... a large proportion of carbon (up to 40% Cmol Cmol-1) was directed to th
growth_records.notes: Engineered strain, autotrophic (knallgas) fed-batch on CO2/H2. Measured (is_experimental:true): 1,3-BDO titer 2.97 g/L and specific carbon production rate ~0.4 Cmol/Cmol/h; up to 40% Cmol/Cmol carbon to product. Substrate (CO2/H2/O2) uptake rates and growth rate/mu NOT reported numerically, so qS and mu are not derivable. Oxygen classed microaerophilic because DO deliberately held low (10%) in a H2/O2 knallgas mix (facultative lithoautotroph). Ferric ammonium citrate amount and full SL-6 composition present in Methods but exact ferric citrate concentration not captured. Best autotrophic strain shown in Fig 9 (H16Delta1::56-p14); abstract attributes 2.97 g/L to the final engineered strain.

literature-collected (Table 1)

product_yields.PHB: non-growth-associated production scenario; positive cash flow when 40% (mol) of glucose used for growth phase (Table 8, simulated)

growth_records.cite: de Oliveira Santos W et al. Analysis of Poly-3-Hydroxybutyrate Production Potential ... via DySEEP (Dynamic Simulations for Evaluation of Economic Potential). ACS Omega (2025). DOI:10.1021/acsomega.4c11178. [PMC12242656]
growth_records.snippet: C. necator aerobic νglc,max 3, νO2,max 5, Kglc 0.015 mmol/L, Ki,glc 11139.0 mmol/L (Table 1, Parameters Used for the DFBA Simulations)
growth_records.notes: MODELING/techno-economic paper (DFBA). Cupriavidus necator (DSM 545 / H16). Table 1 uptake parameters are literature-collected MAXIMA (νglc,max), NOT measured in this paper → is_experimental:false. Kinetics Kglc=0.015 mmol/L, Ki,glc=11139.0 mmol/L. Mineral medium from Table 3. No growth rate/temperature/pH reported. S. cerevisiae (the third case-study organism) excluded as it is a eukaryote.
